name: @chbrain/khai-engine-shame
description: khai engine content, the shame domain: shame as process, how a persona appraises exposure against a social standard and the appraisal produces a response directed at the self or at the one who saw.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Shame as process: how a persona appraises exposure against a social standard, and the appraisal becomes a response directed at the self or at the one who saw -- guilt, embarrassment, or humiliation.

card.wire: Attaches at Process. The root (process_shame.md) declares shame as a social appraisal: the self is seen against a standard, the gap is exposed, and the response is aimed at concealment, repair, recovery, or rage.
card.issue: Three readings a persona may run: guilt (act condemned, repair possible), embarrassment (mild exposure, recoverable), and humiliation (verdict imposed by power, rage secondary). Each is a different resolution of the same social appraisal.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the root. The link: each persona links, under Projection, the specific reading it runs in the scene.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and each persona links a real shame reading. Whether the reading is true to the persona's situation is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the root. Then give the persona its read under Projection, linking the specific expression. Read it in the first beat; the room enacts it, never narrates it.
